source: IEC 61439-1/2/3/4, NEC Article 408, BS 7671 colloquial terminology

board_types.name: Consumer Unit (CU)
board_types.notes: UK domestic terminology. Always has integral main switch + RCD/RCBO arrangement.

board_types.name: Distribution Board (DB)
board_types.notes: Sub-distribution to floors / zones / departments. Typically fed from MSB or floor riser.

board_types.name: Main Switchboard (MSB)
board_types.notes: Service entrance assembly. Fed from utility transformer. Distributes to sub-DBs.

board_types.name: Panelboard (NEMA)
board_types.typical_form: N/A (NEMA Type 1 / 3R / 12 instead of IEC Form)
board_types.notes: US distribution board equivalent. Single-section (lighting/branch) or two-section (lighting + power). Per NEC 408 maximum 42 overcurrent devices per section.

board_types.name: Motor Control Center (MCC)
board_types.notes: Houses motor starters, VFDs, soft-starters. Each starter in own compartment for safe maintenance.

board_types.name: Motor DB (small)
board_types.notes: Local distribution feeding 2-6 small motors. Less elaborate than MCC.
